title: Kena Upanishad II: It Is Not What We Know
description: From the Kena Upanishad II — one of the foundational texts of Indian philosophy.
introduction: From the Kena Upanishad II — one of the foundational texts of Indian philosophy.

translation.text: If you think you know it well, you do not know it. The form of Brahman is unknown to you. He who thinks he knows it, knows it not. He who thinks he knows it not, knows it. It is not understood by those who understand it; it is understood by those who do not understand it.
